lines.en: Yes, in the end it was really restful.

days.title: Le congiunzioni e, ma, o, perché
days.objective: Join short sentences with e, ma, o and perché to build longer, more natural answers.
days.paretoFocus: Short disconnected sentences sound like a beginner, and four small joining words immediately double the length and fluency of everything you say.
drills: Take six short sentences you can already say and join them into three longer ones.
Answer three why questions about yourself, each with a reason introduced by the causal word.
Write two sentences that contrast two ideas, using the contrast word.

items: Which word introduces a reason, and which one introduces a contrast?
Say 'I am not going out because it is raining' in Italian.
How is the question word for why spelled and accented?

activities.title: E, ma, o, perché
activities.intro: E adds, ma contrasts, o offers a choice, and perché gives a reason. Perché with the acute accent is also the question word for why, so the same spelling serves both the question and the answer. Italian keeps the normal word order after all four of these.

items.hr: Studio l'italiano e lavoro in un ufficio.
items.en: I study Italian and I work in an office.
items.note: e simply adds

items.hr: Mi piace il mare, ma preferisco la montagna.
items.en: I like the sea, but I prefer the mountains.
items.note: ma introduces the contrast

items.hr: Prendi il caffè o il tè?
items.en: Are you having coffee or tea?
items.note: o offers a choice

items.hr: Perché non sei venuto ieri?
items.en: Why did you not come yesterday?
items.note: question word, acute accent on the e

items.hr: Non sono uscito perché ha piovuto tutto il giorno.
items.en: I did not go out because it rained all day.
items.note: same word introduces the reason

activities.title: Unire le frasi
activities.intro: Practise joining ideas with the right connector for the meaning.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Voglio uscire, ___ devo studiare.' (I want to go out, but I have to study.)
questions.explanation: The two ideas are in contrast, so ma is the correct connector.

questions.prompt: How is the word for because and why written in Italian?
questions.explanation: It carries an acute accent on the final e, which is different from the grave accent in caffè.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Sono stanco ___ ho dormito poco.' (I am tired because I slept little.)
questions.explanation: The reason follows the causal connector, and the accent must be acute.

questions.prompt: Complete the choice: 'Andiamo al cinema ___ a teatro?' (Are we going to the cinema or to the theatre?)
questions.explanation: Oppure is the fuller form used to offer an alternative, and the short form is also correct here.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'I am not going out because it is raining.'
questions.explanation: Non precedes the verb and the reason follows perché with normal word order.

activities.title: Perché non vieni?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend invites you out and you answer with joined sentences and reasons.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Vieni al concerto stasera o resti a casa?
lines.en: Are you coming to the concert tonight or staying at home?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Resto a casa, perché sono molto stanco.
lines.en: I am staying at home, because I am very tired.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.en: What a shame, you like the music.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Mi piace, ma ieri ho lavorato fino a tardi.
lines.en: I like it, but yesterday I worked until late.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.en: Shall we do something tomorrow then?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sì, domani sono libero e possiamo uscire a cena.
lines.en: Yes, tomorrow I am free and we can go out to dinner.

days.title: Presentarsi all'esame, la prova orale
days.objective: Introduce yourself confidently in a speaking test and answer the standard opening questions about your life, your routine and your recent past.
days.paretoFocus: The first two minutes of a speaking test are always the same three or four questions, and a rehearsed answer that uses correct agreement earns marks straight away.
drills: Prepare and say aloud a sixty second self introduction covering name, origin, home, work or study and family.
Answer these three questions out loud: what you do on a normal day, what you like doing, what you did last weekend.
Record yourself once, then repeat and fix any participle that did not agree.

items: Give three sentences that open a self introduction.
How do you ask the examiner politely to repeat a question?
Which two grammar points should you check while speaking about the past?

activities.title: Mi presento
activities.intro: A speaking test at this level opens with a personal introduction and a few simple questions. Answer in full sentences, not single words, and use the connectors and the past tense you have just learned. If you do not understand, ask politely rather than staying silent.

items.hr: Buongiorno, mi chiamo Elena e ho ventotto anni.
items.en: Good morning, my name is Elena and I am twenty eight.
items.note: chiamarsi is reflexive

items.hr: Sono di Napoli, ma abito a Torino da due anni.
items.en: I am from Naples, but I have been living in Turin for two years.
items.note: di for origin, a for the city where you live

items.hr: Lavoro in un negozio e la sera studio l'italiano.
items.en: I work in a shop and in the evening I study Italian.

items.hr: Mi piace molto cucinare, soprattutto il fine settimana.
items.en: I really like cooking, especially at the weekend.
items.note: piacere plus infinitive stays singular

items.hr: Scusi, può ripetere la domanda, per favore?
items.en: Excuse me, could you repeat the question, please?
items.note: polite request with potere

activities.title: Le domande della prova orale
activities.intro: Practise the phrases and forms that the opening of a speaking test always requires.

questions.prompt: Which is the best full answer to the question 'Come si chiama?'
options: Mi chiamo Elena e sono di Napoli.
questions.answer: Mi chiamo Elena e sono di Napoli.
questions.explanation: A full sentence with a connector shows more language than a single word, and chiamarsi needs its reflexive word.

questions.prompt: How do you politely ask the examiner to repeat something?
questions.explanation: Scusi plus the polite form of potere is the standard courteous request.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Mi ___ Marco e abito a Roma.' (My name is Marco and I live in Rome.)
questions.explanation: Chiamarsi is reflexive, so the io form keeps its normal ending after mi.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Studio l'italiano ___ due anni.' (I have been studying Italian for two years.)
questions.explanation: Da plus a length of time expresses an action that started in the past and still continues.

questions.prompt: Build the Italian for: 'I am from Naples, but I live in Turin.'
questions.explanation: Di marks origin, a marks the city of residence, and ma joins the contrast.

activities.title: La prova orale
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. An examiner opens the speaking test and you answer in full sentences using the grammar of this level.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Buongiorno, si accomodi. Come si chiama?
lines.en: Good morning, take a seat. What is your name?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Buongiorno. Mi chiamo Elena Rossi e ho ventotto anni.
lines.en: Good morning. My name is Elena Rossi and I am twenty eight.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Di dove è e dove abita adesso?
lines.en: Where are you from and where do you live now?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sono di Napoli, ma abito a Torino da due anni.
lines.en: I am from Naples, but I have been living in Turin for two years.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Che cosa fa in una giornata normale?
lines.en: What do you do on a normal day?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Mi alzo alle sette, lavoro fino alle sei e poi studio un po'.
lines.en: I get up at seven, I work until six and then I study a bit.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E che cosa ha fatto lo scorso fine settimana?
lines.en: And what did you do last weekend?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sono andata al mare con un'amica e siamo tornate domenica sera.
lines.en: I went to the seaside with a friend and we came back on Sunday evening.
